## Original task

Deep research on Shenzhen for foreign tourists. Target depth: at least 15 distinct references, at least 30 minutes of research, comprehensive coverage.

Save the final report to: c:/Users/h/Documents/New project 2/v2/research/city-shenzhen-deep.md

This populates a foreign-tourist site's Shenzhen page. Cover these dimensions thoroughly:

## 1. District-by-district breakdown (10 districts)
For EACH of Shenzhen's main 10 districts (Futian, Luohu, Yantian, Nanshan, Bao'an, Longgang, Longhua, Pingshan, Guangming, Dapeng) document:
- District name English + Chinese
- Why a foreign tourist would or would not visit
- Defining character (CBD / tech / port / coastal / suburban / industrial)
- Top 1-3 attractions or sub-areas (Shekou Sea World, OCT-LOFT, Window of the World, Splendid China, Dapeng Fortress, Yantian beaches)
- Food strength: Hakka / Cantonese / migrant cuisines from across China
- Transport hub status (Shenzhen North HSR / Futian HSR / SZX airport / Luohu border / Futian border)
- Tier classification: must-visit / worth-it / skip-on-first-trip with reasoning

## 2. Top 12-15 attractions for foreign tourists
Each attraction must include:
- Name English + Chinese
- District it sits in
- One-paragraph description of why a foreign tourist visits
- Booking method (passport-friendly)
- Daily limit / peak crowd warning
- Opening hours + closed-day pattern
- Foreign-card-acceptance status
- English-language tour / signage status
- Official URL (gov.cn / english.sz.gov.cn / eyeshenzhen.com / official institution domain)
- A public-domain or Creative Commons image URL (Wikimedia Commons preferred). Verify URL exists.

Examples worth covering: Window of the World, Splendid China + Folk Culture Village, OCT-LOFT, Sea World (Shekou), Dameisha + Xiaomeisha beaches, Dapeng Fortress + Dapeng Peninsula, Lianhuashan Park (Deng Xiaoping statue), Shenzhen Bay Park (HK skyline view), Mangrove Conservation Area, Shenzhen Museum, China Folk Culture Village, OCT Harbour, Shenzhen Civic Center, Pingshan Art Museum, Coastal City.

## 3. Cross-border tourism (HK ↔ SZ)
Five border crossings: Luohu, Futian (also FMT high-speed rail), Huanggang, Shekou (ferry), Shenzhen Bay. Times, peak congestion, foreign-passport-vs-mainland-Chinese-passport practical differences. AlipayHK + Shenzhen Tong + Hu Tong Xing card setup. SZ shopping / dining day-trip from HK pattern.

## 4. Food strengths
Hakka cuisine (salt-baked chicken, stuffed tofu) in Yantian/Pingshan; migrant cuisines in Nanshan/Futian; HK-style dim sum in Luohu (cross-border influence); coastal seafood in Shekou and Yantian. Best foreigner-friendly districts.

## 5. Day-trip worth-it
Dapeng Peninsula (full day, beaches + coastal hike), Hong Kong (cross-border, separate guide), Macau (via HZMB, less common from SZ), Huizhou (mainland HSR 30 min), Guangzhou (HSR 45 min).

## 6. Practical pitfalls specific to Shenzhen
- Octopus card does NOT work on Shenzhen Metro (only AlipayHK QR or Shenzhen Tong)
- Border-crossing waits during HK holidays
- AlipayHK vs mainland Alipay confusion
- Shenzhen Metro foreign-card POS only at service centers, not at gates
- Universal Studios is in Beijing, not Shenzhen (common confusion with Window of the World)
- Tap water also not potable

## Sources
- 15+ DISTINCT URLs minimum
- Mix: at least 8 official (sz.gov.cn, eyeshenzhen.com, jtys.sz.gov.cn for transport, attraction official domains) and at most 7 traveller-community references
- Each citation: URL + author/publisher + date + reliability note
- For images: ONLY public-domain or CC-licensed sources. Verify URLs.

## Format
Same as the other 3 cities: structured attraction format with all fields.

Confirm path + total finding count + total distinct URLs at end.

v2/src/data\food.ts:19:      en: "Peking roast duck (Quanjude / Bianyifang traditions). Old-Beijing snacks: noodles with soybean paste, dou-zhi (fermented mung bean), tanghulu, copper-pot mutton hotpot. Halal options well-established.",

v2/src/data\food.ts:51:      en: "Benbang (Shanghainese): braised pork in soy, sweet-soy ribs, drunken chicken. Xiaolongbao (Nanxiang Mantou Dian, Ding Tai Fung style). Strong international cuisine scene around Bund and former French Concession.",
v2/src/data\food.ts:55:    districts: {
v2/src/data\food.ts:61:      en: "Same as Beijing — Meituan/Eleme need a Chinese mobile + domestic payment. Hotels often have curated room-service menus in English as a workaround.",

v2/src/data\food.ts:118:    cityZh: "深圳",
v2/src/data\food.ts:120:      en: "Hakka cuisine (salt-baked chicken, stuffed tofu) plus China-wide migrant cuisines — Sichuan, Hunan, Northeastern. Heavy Cantonese influence in older districts.",

v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:34:    rightsEn: "Can post answers; cannot yet display a city-resident badge.",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:65:  cityScope?: "beijing" | "shanghai" | "guangzhou" | "shenzhen";
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:83:    cityScope: "beijing",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:86:      answererCity: "Beijing · 9 yrs",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:87:      bodyEn: "Last train from PEK T2/T3 to Dongzhimen leaves around 22:50. After that, take the airport shuttle bus or a Beijing B-plate taxi from the official rank — your hotel can confirm fare before you flag one.",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:96:    questionEn: "Severe peanut allergy — does the Mandarin allergy card actually work in small Shanghai restaurants?",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:99:    cityScope: "shanghai",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:102:      answererCity: "Shanghai · 4 yrs",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:112:    questionEn: "Coming from HK — what's the actual cheapest card setup for 3 days in Shenzhen?",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:115:    cityScope: "shenzhen",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:118:      answererCity: "Shenzhen · 6 yrs",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:119:      bodyEn: "Skip Octopus — doesn't work on SZ Metro. Three days = activate Alipay transit QR for Shenzhen (no deposit, refund unused balance). Need physical card? Shenzhen Tong at any service centre, ¥20 deposit refundable.",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:132:  cityEn: string;
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:133:  cityZh: string;
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:146:    cityEn: "Guangzhou",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:147:    cityZh: "广州",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:149:    textEn: "Three things I wish I'd known before flying to Guangzhou: (1) Canton Fair was on, restaurant prices were 40% up — book Pazhou hotels weeks early. (2) The yum cha tea-seat fee surprised me until a local explained — it's normal, not a scam. (3) Metro foreign-card tap actually works fine since October 2025; was way easier than I read in 2024 blogs.",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:158:    cityEn: "Shanghai",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:159:    cityZh: "上海",
v2/src/data\phase2-preview.ts:209:    bodyEn: "Approved → published with city tag, country code, and \"visitor note · authored by [alias] · not officially verified\" badge. Rejected → email with reason.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:8:  cityScope?: CityId[];
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:23:    bodyEn: "New York → Shanghai → New York is denied at immigration. New York → Shanghai → Singapore is approved. Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan count as separate regions for this rule.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:48:    cityScope: ["beijing", "shanghai"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:51:    bodyEn: "240-hour transit travellers must use the TWOV counter, not general immigration. Pudong T2 marks it clearly; Beijing T3 barely labels it — look for the small \"Visa-Free Transit\" sign.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:60:    bodyEn: "SmartShanghai staff tried — rejected for \"insufficient reason\". Processing takes up to 7 working days, so you must apply at least 8 days before expiry. Plan your trip length up front.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:61:    bodyZh: "SmartShanghai 编辑亲测被拒，理由 \"insufficient reason\"。审批最长 7 个工作日，要试至少提前 8 天。出发前就把行程时长定好。",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:100:    cityScope: ["beijing"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:101:    titleEn: "Beijing Metro tap-and-go: only at end-of-row gate lanes",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:108:    id: "transport-alipay-qr-percity",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:110:    titleEn: "Alipay transit QR: must activate per city",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:112:    bodyEn: "Activating Beijing's transit code does NOT activate Shanghai / Guangzhou / Shenzhen. Open the Transport tab, pick the target city, and activate before reaching the turnstile.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:139:    bodyEn: "Auto-collection machines only read Chinese ID cards. Foreign passport holders must queue at staffed windows. At Beijing West / Shanghai Hongqiao that's 20–30 min on a busy day.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:155:    cityScope: ["beijing"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:156:    titleEn: "Beijing has 5+ rail stations — book the right one",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:158:    bodyEn: "Beijing main, West, South, North, Chaoyang. Different stations are 30–60 min apart by metro. Confirm the specific station name when booking, not just \"Beijing\".",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:165:    cityScope: ["beijing"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:168:    bodyEn: "Men posing as helpers near the T3 taxi info desk single out foreign-looking travellers and quote 3–4× meter. Legitimate Beijing taxis show \"Jing B\" plates. Anyone who approaches you first isn't legit — walk to the official taxi queue.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:175:    cityScope: ["guangzhou"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:176:    titleEn: "Black taxi touts at Guangzhou South / East stations — 3–5× meter",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:194:    cityScope: ["shenzhen"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:195:    titleEn: "Octopus card does NOT work on Shenzhen Metro",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:197:    bodyEn: "Hong Kong tourists assume Octopus works — it doesn't on Shenzhen Metro. Options: (1) Alipay transit QR activated for Shenzhen; (2) Shenzhen Tong physical card (CNY 20 deposit); (3) \"Hu Tong Xing\" dual-card sold in Shenzhen for CNY 68 — has both Octopus (HKD) and Shenzhen Tong (CNY) purses, each topped up separately.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:204:    cityScope: ["shanghai"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:205:    titleEn: "Shanghai metro refunds: only 11:00–14:00 at pink service desk",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:214:    cityScope: ["shanghai"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:215:    titleEn: "Shanghai street taxi: stick to yellow (Qiangsheng) or white (Jinjiang)",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:262:    cityScope: ["beijing", "shanghai", "guangzhou"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:272:    cityScope: ["beijing", "shanghai", "guangzhou"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:291:    cityScope: ["guangzhou"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:321:    cityScope: ["beijing", "shanghai"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:331:    cityScope: ["beijing", "shanghai"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:334:    bodyEn: "Beijing United Family / PUMCH international wing / Raffles Shanghai have English-speaking staff but outpatient registration alone is CNY 300–600 vs CNY 10–30 at standard public departments. Without direct-billing insurance, one visit can be CNY 2,000–5,000+.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:350:    cityScope: ["beijing"],
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:351:    titleEn: "Beijing has 3 emergency numbers — 999 sometimes faster than 120",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:353:    bodyEn: "Beijing: 120 (public emergency), 999 (Red Cross / private ambulance, faster in some districts), 110 (police). US Embassy medical assistance +86-10-8531-4000. Beijing United 24h ER +86-10-5927-7120. PUMCH 24h ER +86-10-69155288.",
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:359:export function pitfallsForPillar(pillar: PillarId, cityId?: CityId): Pitfall[] {
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:362:    if (!p.cityScope) return true;
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:363:    if (!cityId) return true;
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:364:    return p.cityScope.includes(cityId);
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:368:export function pitfallSourceIds(pillar?: PillarId, cityId?: CityId): string[] {
v2/src/data\practical-other.ts:369:  const items = pillar ? pitfallsForPillar(pillar, cityId) : pitfalls;
v2/src/data\practical-payments.ts:83:    bodyEn: "Both Alipay and WeChat compare GPS (Beijing) against IP location (e.g. Los Angeles). The mismatch triggers fraud flags that can freeze the account mid-KYC and require a manual support ticket to recover.",

v2/research\alipay-wechat-foreigner-registration.md:6:Cities in scope: Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Shenzhen.
v2/research\alipay-wechat-foreigner-registration.md:37:- Author/publication: LTL Mandarin School blog (language school with Shanghai/Beijing campuses)
v2/research\alipay-wechat-foreigner-registration.md:220:Alipay fraud detection compares GPS location (Beijing) against IP geolocation (e.g., Los Angeles via VPN) and treats the mismatch as suspicious. Especially dangerous during the initial KYC flow: a frozen account during passport upload requires a manual support ticket. Fix: disable VPN entirely before opening Alipay for setup or payment.
v2/research\alipay-wechat-foreigner-registration.md:280:Hotel staff in Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, and Shenzhen use WeChat daily and routinely assist foreign guests. The scan takes approximately 10 seconds. Hostel common areas and fellow travellers are secondary options.
v2/research\alipay-wechat-foreigner-registration.md:413:Tencent fraud detection actively blocks VPN connections during account setup and WeChat Pay verification, flagging IP geolocation mismatches. A phone connected through a US-based VPN while physically in Beijing triggers security flags that can freeze the account or demand additional verification. More aggressively enforced during initial setup than during casual browsing.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:12:The 240-hour visa-free transit requires your onward ticket to leave China for a DIFFERENT country than your origin. New York to Shanghai to New York is denied at immigration. New York to Shanghai to Singapore is approved. Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan count as separate regions for this rule.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:24:Author: SmartShanghai editorial | Date: 2025 | City scope: All-of-China
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:27:Visa-free transit travelers must use the dedicated TWOV counter, not the general immigration line. Pudong Terminal 2 marks it clearly; Beijing T3 barely labels it.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:32:SmartShanghai staff attempted an extension and it was rejected for "insufficient reason." Processing takes up to 7 working days, so you must apply at least 8 days before expiry.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:34:Author: SmartShanghai editorial | Date: 2025 | City scope: All-of-China
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:72:Author: SmartShanghai editorial | Date: 2024 | City scope: All-of-China
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:80:In Guangzhou's wholesale market district (Yide Road, Zhanxi Road) and Shanghai street food areas, scammers replace legitimate vendor QR codes with their own. Always verify the merchant name shown in the app after scanning before confirming payment.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:87:**A-15 - Beijing contactless Visa/Mastercard tap works since Sept 2024 but only at specific gate lanes**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:88:Since September 13, 2024, all 490 Beijing subway stations accept contactless Visa/Mastercard tap-and-go. However, designated international-card turnstiles are at specific lane positions (typically at the end of a row). Tapping a regular domestic-only gate still fails.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:90:Author: Beijing Municipal Government | Date: September 2024 | City scope: BJ
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:92:**A-16 - Alipay transit QR code must be activated separately for each city**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:93:The transport QR code in Alipay is city-specific. Activating for Beijing does not activate for Shanghai, Guangzhou, or Shenzhen. Each requires separate in-app setup under the "Transport" tab, selecting the target city before arriving at the turnstile.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:108:Automatic ticket collection machines process Chinese ID cards only. Foreign passport holders must queue at staffed ticket windows. At large stations (Beijing West, Shanghai Hongqiao) this queue can be 20-30 minutes on busy days.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:117:**A-21 - Beijing has 5+ major rail stations; selecting the wrong one is a costly mistake**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:118:Beijing stations: Beijing (main), Beijing West, Beijing South, Beijing North, Beijing Chaoyang. Trains to different destinations depart from different stations 30-60 minutes apart by metro. Verify the specific station when booking, not just the city name.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:122:**A-22 - Black taxi touts at Beijing Capital T3 approach tourists before the official queue**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:123:Men posing as "helpers" near the T3 taxi information desk single out foreign-looking travellers and offer rides at 3-4x meter rates. Legitimate Beijing taxis display "Jing B" (capital B) license plates. Anyone who approaches you first is not legitimate.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:127:**A-23 - Black taxi touts at Guangzhou South Station charge 3-5x normal rates**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:128:Unlicensed cab touts work the main exits of Guangzhou South (high-speed rail) and Guangzhou East stations. Use DiDi from the designated app pickup zone inside the station, or walk past all touts to the official metered taxi rank.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:137:**A-25 - Standard Octopus card does NOT work on Shenzhen Metro**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:138:Hong Kong tourists visiting Shenzhen often expect Octopus to work as it does in HK. The standard Octopus card is rejected at Shenzhen Metro turnstiles. Options: (1) Alipay QR code activated for Shenzhen, (2) Shenzhen Tong physical card (20 CNY deposit), (3) "Hu Tong Xing" dual-card purchased in Shenzhen (68 CNY) containing both an Octopus purse (HKD) and Shenzhen Tong purse (CNY) - but each must be topped up separately in the relevant city.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:140:Author: EyeShenzhen / Shenzhen government | Date: April 2024 | City scope: SZ
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:142:**A-26 - Shanghai metro refunds only processed at service windows 11:00-14:00 daily**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:143:If you buy a wrong ticket at a Shanghai metro vending machine, refunds are only processed at pink service counters during 11:00-14:00. Outside those hours there is no cash refund. Bring original ticket and passport.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:147:**A-27 - Shanghai taxi reliability: stick to yellow (Qiangsheng) or white (Jinjiang) companies**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:148:Other colors (red, dark blue, or other liveries) are smaller companies with fewer accountability mechanisms. DiDi is preferable, but if flagging a street taxi in Shanghai, stick to yellow or white.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:190:**A-35 - Guangzhou Canton Fair period (April/October) causes price spikes and fake restaurant bookings**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:214:International-standard private hospitals (Beijing United Family, PUMCH international wing, Raffles Hospital Shanghai) offer English-speaking staff, but outpatient registration alone costs 300-600 CNY vs 10-30 CNY at standard public departments. Without international health insurance with direct billing, a single appointment can cost 2,000-5,000+ CNY.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:223:**A-41 - Beijing has 3 emergency numbers; 999 (private ambulance) is sometimes faster than 120**
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:224:Beijing emergency: 120 (public emergency medical), 999 (Red Cross/private ambulance, faster in some districts), 110 (police). US Embassy medical assistance: +86-10-8531-4000. Beijing United Family 24h: +86-10-5927-7120. PUMCH 24h emergency: +86-10-69155288.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:226:Author: Beijing Municipal Government | Date: 2024 | City scope: BJ
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:260:- Popular destinations by city
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:262:Content types: Booking widgets (primary), editorial how-to guides, city overviews, practical articles.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:263:Entry-point user flow: Landing page -> destination search bar -> city selection -> service type tabs. Practical guides via "Travel Guide" mega-menu item.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:269:Primary user type: Activity and experience booking; strong in day tours and attraction tickets.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:271:Activity categories for Beijing and Shanghai:
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:272:1. Attraction tickets (Forbidden City, Great Wall, Shanghai Tower, Shanghai Disney)
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:277:6. Theme parks (Universal Beijing Resort)
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:280:Entry-point user flow: Homepage search or city browse -> activity list with filters -> product detail -> booking. No destination guide layer between search and purchase.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:281:Structural note: Primary differentiator is instant confirmation ticketing for sold-out attraction slots. No practical guidance content (payment, transport, medical).
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:288:Category filters for Beijing:
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:290:2. Day trips from Beijing
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:296:Entry-point user flow: Destination search -> city listing with activity cards -> filters -> product page -> checkout.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:309:3. Cities: Beijing, Shanghai, Hong Kong featured; Guangzhou and Shenzhen sparse
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:314:Content types: Long-form editorial articles, city guides, itineraries, accommodation recommendations, bookable tours.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:315:Entry-point user flow: Site header -> Asia section -> China country page -> editorial content by city or theme -> bookable trip or guidebook purchase.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:316:Structural note: Most content is text-editorial with no booking widgets on the page. Guidebook upsell is prominent. Guangzhou and Shenzhen city pages are sparse.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:332:Secondary categories within City Guide: top attractions, culture topics, travel tips (visa, money, scam avoidance), maps and transport guides.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:376:Content types: Bespoke itineraries, blog/travelogue articles, documentary short films ("Bendi Stories"), podcast content, city pre-departure resource guides.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:405:### C-1: Beijing
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:407:Operator: Beijing Municipal Bureau of Culture and Tourism (Promotional Centre)
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:412:- EXPLORE (attractions, leisure, accommodation, food, time-honored brands, itinerary routes)
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:425:Recency: Active - May 2026 event listings present, live weather widget, ticketing integration with major attractions.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:432:- Direct attraction ticket booking portal (english.beijing.gov.cn/specials/ticketing/)
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:435:Multiple portals: Yes. visitbeijing.com.cn is the tourism-bureau promotional site. english.beijing.gov.cn/travellinginbeijing is the city government practical resource. Both maintained.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:439:### C-2: Shanghai
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:441:Operator: Shanghai Municipal Administration of Culture and Tourism
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:467:Multiple portals: Yes. meet-in-shanghai.net is the tourism bureau promotional site. english.shanghai.gov.cn/en-GuideforForeignTourists is the city government practical guide - the more structured resource for pre-trip logistics.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:471:### C-3: Guangzhou
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:490:Language quality: Competent government-style English. Some phrasing suggests translation from Chinese originals. Generally clear and usable. Less polished than Beijing and Shanghai equivalents.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:494:- Explicit consulate contact directory for foreign nationals in Guangzhou
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:500:Multiple portals: The International portal at gz.gov.cn is the main English gateway. No separate tourism bureau promotional site equivalent to visitbeijing.com.cn or meet-in-shanghai.net was found operational. The Guangzhou portal is noticeably thinner in tourist-specific content than Beijing and Shanghai equivalents. This is a coverage gap to address on our own site.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:504:### C-4: Shenzhen
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:506:Operator: Shenzhen government international web portal
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:515:- EyeShenzhen and Friends (expat story video column)
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:531:Language quality: Professional English on both portals. EyeShenzhen is well-maintained with clear categorisation. Some event listings show mixed freshness.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:532:Recency: EyeShenzhen shows February 2026 events current; Service Guide Videos dated July 2024 (slightly dated). sz.gov.cn travel section notes "as of end of 2025" for travel agency data.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:536:- "A Guide for Expats Visiting Shenzhen" covering entry visa, payment, departure tax refund, telecom
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:539:- Cross-border transport card guide (Octopus / Hu Tong Xing / Lingnan Pass) - only city bureau to address the HK-Shenzhen card interoperability question directly
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:542:Multiple portals: Yes. eyeshenzhen.com is the flagship international portal. sz.gov.cn/en_szgov/travel is a more basic supplementary section. EyeShenzhen is stronger than most city equivalents in practical foreigner-specific content.
v2/research\blog-platform-bureau-sweep.md:550:- Section C city bureaus: 4 cities, 8 portals total (2 per city)
v2/research\ugc-qa-implementation.md:91:### B2. Reddit city flair: moderator-granted, no platform standard, lightweight and viable
v2/research\ugc-qa-implementation.md:92:Reddit flair system lets moderators with Manage flair permission assign user flair by username search and label assignment. On city subreddits (r/shanghai, r/beijing), some moderators grant local resident flair but each subreddit sets its own verification criteria -- ranging from post proof in modmail to just ask. Reddit launched grey-checkmark verified profiles in December 2025, but this is invitation-only for public figures and brands, not local-resident verification. The mod-grant model is a viable, zero-infrastructure pattern for a small platform.
v2/research\ugc-qa-implementation.md:130:The Guangzhou Internet Court September 2024 PIPL ruling confirms cross-border personal data transfer requires separate, unbundled consent naming the foreign receiver. For a startup, the risk-to-benefit ratio of collecting any Chinese government ID data is prohibitive.
v2/research\ugc-qa-implementation.md:181:If a post can identify a person directly or indirectly, GDPR treats translation, storage, alteration, dissemination, and making available as processing. GDPR consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous, though contract or legitimate interest may sometimes be the lawful basis for ordinary publishing. For Mainland China users, PIPL cross-border rules are stricter: Article 39 requires notice of the overseas recipient, purpose, method, categories of personal information, rights procedures, and separate consent for cross-border transfer; the Guangzhou Internet Court's 2024 decision treated bundled general consent as insufficient. The checkbox should cover copyright permission, while the privacy notice covers data processing and overseas vendors.
v2/research\ugc-qa-implementation.md:206:Stack Overflow's core quality loop is voting plus reputation: upvotes signal useful, well-researched posts; downvotes signal wrong or poorly researched posts; reputation then unlocks moderation capacity. Editing is the maintenance layer because posts can be corrected and improved without replacing the answer. Hard answer expiry is a bad default for travel/policy Q&A because old answers may still apply; prefer "last verified" and "needs update" states over hiding answers solely by age.

 succeeded in 676ms:
# Alipay & WeChat Pay: Practical Pitfalls for Foreign Tourists in China

Research compiled: 2026-05-09
Scope: Operational gotchas NOT covered by official PBOC/Alipay/WeChat documentation.
Coverage: Registration, KYC, card binding, merchant rejection, account suspension, fallback strategy.
Cities in scope: Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Shenzhen.

---

## PART 1 - ALIPAY

### 1.1 Which version to install

**Finding A-1: ONE app for foreign tourists - standard Alipay app (blue icon)**

The old Alipay Tour Pass mini-program has been superseded. The original Alipay International standalone app no longer exists. Foreign tourists should download the standard Alipay app (publisher: Hangzhou Technology Co., Ltd., bundle ID com.eg.android.AlipayGphone on Android, App Store ID 333206289 on iOS). AlipayHK is a completely separate product for Hong Kong residents only and does NOT work for mainland China payments.

- URL: https://www.wanderinchina.com/survival-guide/useful-mobile-apps/alipay/
- Author/publication: WanderinChina travel guide (updated March 2026)
- Date: March 2026
- Credibility: Long-running expat travel guide, post explicitly dated March 2026.

**Finding A-2: Tour Card (formerly Tour Pass) being shut down - May 29, 2026**

The Alipay Tour Card mini-program stops accepting new card applications on May 29, 2026. Existing accounts can still top up temporarily. The product is being migrated to the UnionPay app or Nihao China app. Any content recommending Tour Card as the primary setup method is now outdated.

- URL: https://chinaguidelines.com/en/posts/tour-card
- Author/publication: ChinaGuidelines.com (foreign-tourist China travel guide)
- Date: 2026 (covers May 2026 shutdown)
- Credibility: Specific shutdown date cited; aligns with multiple corroborating sources.

**Finding A-3: Tour Pass 90-day cap vs. Tour Card 1-year cap - confusion in older guides**

Two products existed side by side with conflicting documentation. The older Tour Pass expired after 90 days and could not be extended. The newer Tour Card was valid for one year from issuance. Many blog posts conflate the two. As of 2026 both are being wound down in favour of direct foreign-card binding.

- URL: https://ltl-school.com/alipay-for-foreigners/
- Author/publication: LTL Mandarin School blog (language school with Shanghai/Beijing campuses)
- Date: 2026
- Credibility: Practitioner travel blog from an established China-based institution.

---

### 1.2 Registration and KYC with a foreign passport

**Finding A-4: Real-name verification is mandatory and blocks ALL payments until complete**

Foreign users must submit passport photos and pass a face-liveness scan before any transaction is allowed. Verification typically completes in 1-10 minutes but can take up to 24 hours during high-load periods. Transactions above USD 500 always require completed KYC; below that threshold some functions are available without it.

- URL: https://hiddenchinatravel.com/alipay-wechat-pay-verification-failed/
- Author/publication: Hidden China Travel (China-specialist travel agency blog)
- Date: 2025-2026
- Credibility: Detailed technical breakdown of failure categories; corroborated by multiple other sources.

**Finding A-5: Four specific KYC failure categories - passport photo quality is the most common**

Four distinct failure points: (1) passport photo quality - glare on laminate, shadows, cropped edges; (2) name format mismatches - Western middle names, hyphenated surnames, names differing between passport MRZ and bank card; (3) phone/SMS issues - eSIM data access does not guarantee SMS receipt; bank OTP may be sent to your home number, not your travel SIM; (4) risk controls - VPN active during KYC triggers flags, as does registering from a new device.

- URL: https://hiddenchinatravel.com/alipay-wechat-pay-verification-failed/
- Author/publication: Hidden China Travel
- Date: 2025-2026
- Credibility: Most technically detailed breakdown of KYC failure types found across all sources.

**Finding A-6: Verification failed and payment failed require different solutions**

Critical distinction: Verification failed means KYC is incomplete and no transactions are possible. Payment failed means KYC completed but the specific transaction was declined. Treating the wrong failure mode leads users down incorrect troubleshooting paths - e.g., re-uploading passport photos when the real issue is the card issuer blocking the transaction.

- URL: https://hiddenchinatravel.com/alipay-wechat-pay-verification-failed/
- Author/publication: Hidden China Travel
- Date: 2025-2026
- Credibility: Unique distinction not found in official documentation.

**Finding A-7: Name must be uppercase English exactly matching passport MRZ - no partial matches**

The system expects an exact match between the passport MRZ name field and the name entered during registration. Middle names, double-barrelled surnames, and hyphenated names commonly cause rejections. One user reported: "bank card pictures, then saying verification failed and end of story" with no further explanation from the app.

- URL: https://letstraveltochina.com/how-to-use-alipay-in-china/
- Author/publication: LetsTravelToChina.com
- Date: 2026
- Credibility: Travel guide with specific name-format warning backed by user-reported failure.

# Blog / Platform / Bureau Sweep
## Foreign tourist sources beyond .gov.cn - all 5 pillars, 4 cities
### Generated: 2026-05-09 | Research scope: 2024-01 to present

---

## SECTION A - Personal Travel Blogs / Forum Threads / Practical-Pitfall Sources

### PILLAR 1: VISA + ENTRY

**A-01 - Third-country rule silently rejects same-country round-trips**
The 240-hour visa-free transit requires your onward ticket to leave China for a DIFFERENT country than your origin. New York to Shanghai to New York is denied at immigration. New York to Shanghai to Singapore is approved. Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan count as separate regions for this rule.
Source: https://chinafortravelers.com/guides/china-visa-free-transit/
Author: ChinaForTravelers editors | Date: 2025 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-02 - 240-hour clock starts at immigration clearance, not wheels-down**
The 10-day countdown begins when the officer stamps your passport, not when you land. If you land at 23:00 and clear immigration at 00:30, you lose nearly a full day. Many travelers miscalculate and overstay.
Source: https://chinafortravelers.com/guides/china-visa-free-transit/
Author: ChinaForTravelers editors | Date: 2025 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-03 - Digital Arrival Card mandatory since November 2025**
The NIA digital arrival card (s.nia.gov.cn) must be filled within 72 hours before arrival. Missing it causes a secondary desk stop at immigration adding 20-40 minutes. Not mentioned in airline boarding instructions.
Source: https://www.smartshanghai.com/articles/shanghai-life/the-faq-china-visa-on-arrivals-need-to-know
Author: SmartShanghai editorial | Date: 2025 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-04 - Wrong immigration queue adds 30 minutes**
Visa-free transit travelers must use the dedicated TWOV counter, not the general immigration line. Pudong Terminal 2 marks it clearly; Beijing T3 barely labels it.
Source: https://chinafortravelers.com/guides/china-visa-free-transit/
Author: ChinaForTravelers editors | Date: 2025 | City scope: BJ/SH

**A-05 - Extension requests almost always fail; plan before arrival**
SmartShanghai staff attempted an extension and it was rejected for "insufficient reason." Processing takes up to 7 working days, so you must apply at least 8 days before expiry.
Source: https://www.smartshanghai.com/articles/shanghai-life/the-faq-china-visa-on-arrivals-need-to-know
Author: SmartShanghai editorial | Date: 2025 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-06 - Overstay penalty is entry ban, not just a fine**
Overstays beyond a few days trigger a 1-5 year re-entry ban in addition to fines starting at 500 CNY/day.
Source: https://chinafortravelers.com/guides/china-visa-free-transit/
Author: ChinaForTravelers editors | Date: 2025 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-07 - Unlicensed guesthouses cannot legally host foreign guests**
Chinese law requires accommodation to register foreign guests with police. Budget guesthouses and Airbnb-style listings may lack the required license. Both tourist and host face fines.
Source: https://www.thechinajourney.com/china-travel-tips/
Author: TheChina Journey editorial | Date: 2025-2026 | City scope: All-of-China


### PILLAR 2: PAYMENTS

**A-08 - Alipay TourCard does not support American Express**
AmEx is excluded from the Alipay TourCard / international version as of late 2025. Workaround: use a Visa or Mastercard credit card (not debit).
Source: https://wildchina.com/2025/10/guide-to-using-alipay-2025/
Author: WildChina editorial | Date: October 2025 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-09 - Debit cards fail more often than credit cards on Alipay/WeChat**
Visa and Mastercard debit cards often pass the linkage step but silently fail at checkout. The same number as a credit card succeeds. Bring a credit card, not just a debit card.
Source: https://rachelmeetschina.com/2024/10/29/traveling-to-china-how-to-connect-your-foreign-bank-card-to-the-alipay-and-wechat/
Author: Rachel Meets China | Date: October 2024 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-10 - WeChat account can be temporarily restricted by automated security flags**
A traveller's WeChat account was restricted in November 2024 after triggering an automated security check (possibly a login from a new device abroad). Restoration takes 24-72 hours. Set up both Alipay AND WeChat before departure.
Source: https://www.marketplace.org/story/2024/09/26/e-payments-wechat-are-essential-in-china-but-still-tricky-for-foreign-visitors
Author: Marketplace/NPR | Date: September 2024 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-11 - Alipay TourCard charges a 5% conversion fee on every top-up**
On top of whatever foreign transaction fee your home bank charges. For a 5,000 CNY top-up, 250 CNY is lost immediately. Travellers with WeChat Pay linked directly to a card can avoid this fee for WeChat transactions.
Source: https://wildchina.com/2025/10/guide-to-using-alipay-2025/
Author: WildChina editorial | Date: October 2025 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-12 - Many small vendors have no card reader and no change for large notes**
Cash is legally required to be accepted but in practice many street food stalls have no card reader and no change for 100 CNY notes. Carry 10-50 CNY notes as backup.
Source: https://www.smartshanghai.com/articles/essentials/how-to-pay-in-china-as-a-foreign-tourist
Author: SmartShanghai editorial | Date: 2024 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-13 - Revolut and virtual banks work more reliably than traditional banks on Alipay**
Revolut, Wise, and similar fintech-issued Mastercards succeed at a higher rate than traditional high-street bank cards. Some traditional banks block overseas digital-wallet binding by default.
Source: https://rachelmeetschina.com/2024/10/29/traveling-to-china-how-to-connect-your-foreign-bank-card-to-the-alipay-and-wechat/
Author: Rachel Meets China | Date: October 2024 | City scope: All-of-China

**A-14 - WeChat QR code fraud: scammers replace merchant codes at market stalls**
In Guangzhou's wholesale market district (Yide Road, Zhanxi Road) and Shanghai street food areas, scammers replace legitimate vendor QR codes with their own. Always verify the merchant name shown in the app after scanning before confirming payment.
